preview_text:
The model favors Netherlands at 70%, with Tunisia assigned a 9% chance of victory. The underdog's modest win probability reflects a substantial gap in the forecast, though the draw possibility at 22% creates some competitive texture.

The win-draw-loss spread shows Netherlands as clear favorite, but the 22% draw probability suggests the match carries real tightness in parts. Tunisia's 9% win chance leaves little margin for upset; a draw outcome remains plausible enough that the fixture is unlikely to be entirely one-sided in execution, even if the modal result heavily favors the favorite.

Model and market consensus are closely aligned across all three outcomes, with gaps under 3 percentage points. Polymarket prices sit slightly higher on a Netherlands victory and lower on draws, but the divergence is minor. The three sources converge on a Netherlands-favored but contested match.
